Over the past decade, Turkey has quietly become the world's busiest destination for hair restoration. The reasons are not mysterious: a dense concentration of specialised surgeons, a healthcare infrastructure built around international patients, and treatment packages that often cost a fraction of what the same procedure runs in Western Europe or North America. Add a city like Istanbul, where you can recover between two continents, with the Bosphorus on one side and centuries of history on the other, and it becomes clear why so many people turn a medical appointment into a genuine trip.But choosing a clinic from abroad is harder than choosing a hotel. You are trusting a team you have never met with a result you will carry for the rest of your life. This guide ranks ten of the most established hair transplant clinics in Istanbul and pairs the list with practical travel advice, so you can plan both the procedure and the days around it with confidence.The industry's premier medical R&D hub and pioneer of ultra-dense hair restoration.A multidisciplinary board of surgeons and biomedical researchers with over 15 years of combined clinical experience. Vera Clinic is not just a hair transplant centre; it is a recognised research and development institution that actively develops the industry's next-generation technologies. The team is behind innovations, including theCVD lab-grown diamond-tipped instrument for precision micro-incisions andfor maximum density. Named Best Hair Transplant Clinic in Turkey by the European Award in Medicine (2021) , and home to, which trains physicians and runs ongoing clinical research, the clinic gives patients access to patented, next-generation methods inside a five-star facility.Expert-led aesthetic restoration overseen by a Cleveland Clinic-trained plastic surgeon.Directed by Dr Ekrem Keskin, with extensive verified acclaim across major international medical review platforms.Capital Hair Center places a board-certified plastic surgeon at the helm of every procedure, bringing genuine precision to hairline design and facial harmony. Sapphire FUE and DHI are complemented by proprietary treatments like OzonFect and needle-free anaesthesia. A strong option for patients who want their transplant handled with the rigour of reconstructive surgery.A boutique clinic focused on personalised treatment and individual hairline design.Founded in 2016 by Şule Ölmez and supported by an experienced medical team.Unlike larger clinics that perform a high volume of procedures, Sule Hair Transplant emphasises a more personalised patient experience. The clinic offers Sapphire FUE and DHI techniques, with treatment plans tailored to each patient's goals and hair characteristics. Patients who value direct communication, detailed consultation, and a boutique environment often find the clinic's approach appealing. Its all-inclusive packages and structured aftercare programme also make the process straightforward for international visitors.A boutique approach pairing AI mapping technology with low daily patient volumes.A boutique team headed by Dr Merve Kaya.Este Favor focuses on lower daily intakes, so each case receives dedicated attention, pairing AI-assisted planning with the now-familiar combination of treatment plus travel coordination. It is a careful, detail-oriented option for medical tourists who want a smaller-scale experience.A long-standing, multidisciplinary institution with two decades of experience.Boasting a 4.8-star rating from thousands of verified reviews and global recognition in outlets.Aslı Tarcan Clinic draws on two decades of experience, covering Sapphire FUE, DHI, and Robotic DHI, with optional PRP and stem cell therapies. Multilingual staff, VIP transfers, and structured aftercare make it a solid choice for patients seeking a review-backed clinic with a long track record and all-inclusive setup.A straightforward, package-based experience for international medical tourists.An established Istanbul provider working in the international medical tourism space.Medart bundles the procedure with all the practical essentials, consultation, transfers, accommodation guidance, and aftercare that overseas patients look for. It's a strong pick for those comparing several mainstream Istanbul options and wanting a clear, predictable, all-in-one process.One of the largest hair transplant providers in Turkey, serving a high volume of international patients each year.Large multidisciplinary medical team and dedicated international patient services.Elithair has built its reputation around accessibility, structured treatment pathways, and experience managing patients from around the world. The clinic offers FUE-based hair restoration procedures supported by multilingual coordinators, comprehensive treatment packages, and established pre- and post-operative processes. With consultation locations in several countries and a large operational infrastructure, it is often considered by patients looking for a streamlined and highly organised treatment experience.Streamlined, end-to-end care built around the international patient.A coordinated team specialising in travel-and-treatment packages for overseas patients.AHD Clinic delivers a smooth, fully managed journey from initial consultation through aftercare. Its appeal lies in the seamless coordination that has become the Istanbul standard, ideal for patients who want a hassle-free, supported experience from start to finish.World-class Sapphire and DHI expertise on a high-volume scale.A high-volume international practice headed by Dr Levent Acar.Dr Acar is highly respected across Europe, and the clinic has built significant visibility in the international market. Custom-built for international patients, Cosmedica offers seamlessly structured all-inclusive packages using state-of-the-art techniques, making it a go-to for those comparing the larger, well-known practices.Full-service treatment paired with comprehensive logistical support.An Istanbul team focused primarily on serving overseas patients.Transmed combines the surgical procedure with airport transfers, accommodation guidance, and structured follow-up, simplifying the entire experience of travelling abroad for treatment. It suits patients who want both quality care and worry-free logistics in one package.Turkey's position as the world's leading hair transplant destination is not the result of marketing alone. It is built on decades of experience, high patient volumes, specialised medical teams, and continuous investment in hair restoration technologies.For international patients comparing options, the search for the best hair transplant clinic in Turkey often extends beyond price alone and includes factors such as surgeon involvement, patient support, and long-term results.As competition has increased, leading clinics have focused on improving patient outcomes, refining techniques, and delivering more natural-looking results. For patients willing to research their options carefully, Turkey continues to offer a combination of expertise, accessibility, and value that remains difficult to match elsewhere.
Article Summary: Turkey has quietly become the world's busiest destination for hair restoration. A dense concentration of specialised surgeons, a healthcare infrastructure built around international patients, and treatment packages that often cost a fraction of what the same procedure runs in Western Europe or North America. But choosing a clinic from abroad is harder than choosing a hotel.

Article Title: Auto industry analysts are worried about Tesla and other car makers using the technology that Google, Apple, Samsung and other smartphone companies have been using since over ten years to update their
Article Text:
OTA updates raise cybersecurity concerns: Analyst



Norwegian bus test highlights potential risks



Auto makers are increasingly using over-the-air (OTA) software updates, a technology that smartphone companies like Google, Apple and Samsung have relied on for more than a decade to deliver new features and security fixes. While the technology has made updating vehicles faster and cheaper, cybersecurity experts are warning that it also creates new risks. According to a CNBC report, analysts say wireless updates could leave connected vehicles vulnerable to cyberattacks, data breaches and even attempts by foreign actors to interfere with vehicle systems.Over-the-air (OTA) technology allows manufacturers to wirelessly send software updates, bug fixes and new features to internet-connected devices without requiring a visit to a service center.Tesla began rolling out OTA updates for its Model S vehicles in 2012, helping popularize the technology across the automotive industry. Today, many car makers use OTA updates to improve vehicle software and fix issues remotely."The technology is increasingly welcomed as it is a quick and cost-effective way to manage systems on vehicles, over traditional methods which may have required a recall or update at routine maintenance," Siraj Ahmed Shaikh, professor in systems security at Swansea University in the UK, told CNBC.However, analysts say the growing use of OTA updates also introduces new cybersecurity risks.Its use represents "a unique national security concern," Gabriel Lim, senior analyst at the S. Rajaratnam School of International Studies in Singapore, told CNBC."Aside from data privacy concerns, the potential of a foreign actor sabotaging the controls of a moving vehicle is a possibility that countries like Norway, Denmark, and Britain have expressed concerns about," Lim added.The concerns gained attention after Norwegian public transport company Ruter tested two buses last year and found potential security risks linked to OTA technology."There is access to the control system for battery and power supply via mobile network through a Romanian SIM card. In theory, therefore, this bus can be stopped or rendered inoperable by the manufacturer," the company said.The findings prompted authorities in the UK and Denmark to launch their own investigations.
Article Summary: Auto makers are increasingly using over-the-air (OTA) software updates. The technology allows manufacturers to send software updates, bug fixes and new features to internet-connected devices without requiring a visit to a service center. But cybersecurity experts are warning that it also creates new risks.

Article Title: LTM and Glean join forces to unlock enterprise AI value
Article Text: LTM has announced a strategic partnership with Glean to help enterprises scale AI adoption by building a trusted context foundation across business systems. The collaboration will combine LTM’s BlueVerse agentic AI ecosystem with Glean’s enterprise knowledge and intelligence layer, enabling organizations to drive productivity, accelerate decision-making, and achieve outcome-driven results. One of the biggest barriers to enterprise AI success is fragmented knowledge spread across multiple applications and workflows. By leveraging Glean’s enterprise knowledge graph, AI-powered search, assistants, and integrations, employees will gain secure, permission-aware access to the information they need to act quickly and effectively.The partnership will particularly benefit enterprises in banking, financial services, insurance, manufacturing, and other highly regulated sectors. Together, LTM and Glean aim to operationalize AI across IT support, knowledge management, and enterprise application operations, providing a governed path from enterprise search to autonomous, outcome-driven agents.Venu Lambu, CEO and MD of LTM, said: “AI will deliver its greatest impact when it can access the full context of an enterprise and seamlessly connect people, knowledge, and workflows. Our partnership with Glean strengthens LTM’s AI-led transformation portfolio and complements our BlueVerse ecosystem by unlocking intelligence across enterprise systems while maintaining governance, security, and compliance.Amar Maletira, President and COO of Glean, added: “Large enterprises want AI that understands their business, respects governance, and helps employees take action. Pairing Glean’s trusted context layer with LTM’s expertise and BlueVerse ecosystem moves customers from fragmented knowledge to measurable outcomes.”The joint offering is designed to deliver tangible results, including:* Faster incident resolution* Improved employee productivity* Reduced support costs* Accelerated onboarding* Enhanced enterprise search* More efficient application support
Article Summary: LTM has announced a strategic partnership with Glean to help enterprises scale AI adoption by building a trusted context foundation across business systems. The collaboration will combine LTM’s BlueVerse agentic AI ecosystem and Glean's enterprise knowledge and intelligence layer. It will particularly benefit enterprises in banking, financial services, insurance, manufacturing, and other highly regulated sectors.

Article Title: AI Czar David Sacks says China's Kimi K3 model is ‘concerning’ for America: 'This is exactly what I ...', gets response from Elon Musk
Article Text:
This is concerning. For the first time, a Chinese model Kimi K3 has taken #1 on the Frontend Code Arena and is scoring at or near the frontier on other benchmarks

Meanwhile America is tying itself in knots: politicians and bureaucrats are banning new data centers, piling on state regulations, and pushing for new federal agencies to pre-approve frontier models

This is how you lose the AI race. The rest of the world won’t play by our rules if we bog ourselves down. Permissionless innovation is how America won the internet and became the technological envy of the world. We can do it again with AI -- while addressing risks in a targeted way -- or we’ll watch our lead evaporate

Sacks warns of over-regulation



This is *exactly* what I predicted would happen. I said Chinese models would have advanced cyber capabilities within a matter of months and the only thing to do about it was to use AI-powered cyberdefense to protect our systems. Trying to gatekeep models doesn’t work

China’s challenge to America with Kimi K3



AI Czar David Sacks has spoken out on China’s new Kimi K3 artificial intelligence (AI) model, calling its performance “concerning” for American technological dominance. Sacks warned that heavy domestic regulation is crippling US innovation just as overseas rivals catch up, drawing an immediate agreement from billionaire Elon Musk . Sacks comments came soon after Beijing promoted itself as an alternative to American supremacy in AI.,” Sacks said in a post on X (formerly Twitter). Beijing-backed startup Moonshot AI officially open-sourced a massive 2.8 trillion-parameter system that is actively dethroning top American AI models on global coding benchmarks.,” Sacks added, purportedly pointing to US government’s decision to restrict Anthropic’s Fable 5 model to non-Americans.,” he added.Sebastian Mallaby, a senior fellow at the council on foreign relations, challenged Sacks by pointing out the extreme national security risks associated with China open-sourcing such a powerful tool.Mallaby warned that Kimi K3's release means “Mythos-level cyber capability is going to be freely downloadable soon,” putting advanced hacking tools into the hands of anyone with an internet connection.Sacks fired back, arguing that government gatekeeping is completely ineffective against foreign entities.,” Sacks replied.SpaceX and Tesla CEO Elon Musk quickly chimed in to support Sacks’ assessment, replying with a brief: “True.”The technical specifications of Kimi K3 highlight just how rapidly Chinese AI firms, including rivals like DeepSeek, MiniMax, and Z.ai, are closing the gap with Silicon Valley. Backed by major tech conglomerates like Alibaba and Tencent, Moonshot is currently seeking $2 billion in fresh funding at a staggering valuation of $30 billion. Moonshot announced it will release the full model weights and code to the public by July 27.
Article Summary: China's new Kimi K3 artificial intelligence (AI) model has taken #1 on the Frontend Code Arena and is scoring at or near the frontier on other benchmarks. Sacks warned that heavy domestic regulation is crippling US innovation just as overseas rivals catch up.

Article Title: Calling the state 'Home', Dell CEO Michael Dell made one of largest public university donations in US history, 'gifts' $750 million to University of …
Article Text:
Michael Dell’s connection to UT Austin



Michael Dell on Dell's plan to move company's base to Texas

Michael Dell, the founder and CEO of Dell Technologies has pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, marking one of the largest donations ever made to a US public university. The gift will fund a new healthcare and research campus, including the nation’s first AI-native hospital, and expands Dell’s lifetime contributions to UT beyond $1billion. The new campus will feature a hospital designed around artificial intelligence, where AI systems will help doctors detect diseases earlier and also create personalised treatment plans. The facility is expected to open in 2030, positioning UT Austin as a leader in AI-driven medicine and research.Michael Dell’s connection to UT Austin dates back to 1983, when he enrolled as a pre-med student before founding Dell Technologies from his dorm room. Over the years, he and his wife Susan have made major contributions:* 2005: $25 million to build Dell Children’s Medical Center* 2013: $50 million to establish Dell Medical School* 2026: $750 million gift, bringing total donations past $1 billionUT Austin recently renamed Dell’s freshman residence hall “Dell House” in recognition of his enduring legacy.The donation made by Michael Dell will also fund scholarships, advanced computing infrastructure, and medical research initiatives, strengthening UT’s role as a hub for healthcare innovation. Dell emphasised the personal nature of the gift, saying: “I think about this as the next step in a timeline that actually goes back to my parents sending me off to UT to become a doctor. Obviously, that part didn’t work out, but I never stopped thinking about that. ”Last month, Michael Dell said that 97% of Dell shareholders voted in favour of moving the company’s legal base from Delaware to Texas. Dell, who founded the company in Austin in 1984 shared a post of social media platform X (formerly known as Twitter) in which he wrote, “Today, with 97% approval, Dell shareholders voted to bring our legal home to Texas. This is home and where we’ve always belonged. Texas gave us the talent, the universities, and the environment to build something that lasts. Proud to make it official. Let’s go.” This move from the Dell CEO comes days after Texas Governor Gregg Abbott welcomed the company with a “Welcome Home” message.In May this year, Michael Dell said that the company’s “Board of Directors has unanimously approved changing the jurisdiction of Dell Technologies’ state of incorporation from Delaware to its home state of Texas”. This “redomestication” of Dell is likely to be approved at the company’s annual stockholders meeting on June 25 and how the stake holders also voted in favour of the decision. Responding to this decision of Dell, exas Governor Greg Abbott shared an X post welcoming the laptop-maker. “Welcome home, @Dell,” Abbott wrote in the post. adding “For over 40 years, Texas has been where @MichaelDell built and innovated. Now, Dell Technologies is bringing its legal home to Texas.”
Article Summary: Michael Dell, the founder and CEO of Dell Technologies has pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin. The gift will fund a new healthcare and research campus, including the nation’s first AI-native hospital. The new campus will feature a hospital designed around artificial intelligence, where AI systems will help doctors detect diseases earlier.

Article Title: Elon Musk responds as user who shares frustration with Tesla Self-Driving getting stuck in slower traffic, says: Car will start remembering your ...
Article Text:
Tesla user complains about the EV leaving faster lanes

Elon Musk says Tesla will learn from driver behavior

Tesla CEO Elon Musk has said the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system will soon begin remembering each driver's individual preferences. Elon Musk’s remarks come after a user complained that the software keeps leaving carpool and express lanes before getting stuck in slower traffic. Musk said the vehicle will learn from a driver's manual interventions and adjust its behavior over time, suggesting future versions of Tesla's self-driving software will become more personalized.The discussion started after X (formerly Twitter) user Whole Mars Catalog (@WholeMarsBlog) shared a post describing a common frustration while using Tesla's Full Self-Driving system."One thing that really frustrates me is when Tesla Self-Driving exits the carpool lane or express lane and then gets stuck in traffic," the user wrote. "As I see everyone else whizzing by in the far left lane, I get extremely annoyed knowing I should be there too."The user ended the post by asking Tesla's software to respect driver settings, writing, "Respect the settings please...."Responding to the post, Elon Musk said Tesla is working on a feature that will allow the car to adapt to individual driving preferences."The car will start to remember your specific interventions and match each person's individual preferences," Musk wrote on X.His comment suggests that future updates to Tesla's Full Self-Driving software will learn from the times drivers manually override the system, allowing the vehicle to make decisions that better match each owner's driving style.
Article Summary: Elon Musk says Tesla will learn from driver behavior. Tesla CEO said the company's Full Self-Driving system will soon begin remembering each driver's individual preferences. Musk's remarks come after a user complained that the software keeps leaving carpool and express lanes before getting stuck in slower traffic.

Article Title: Philips unite LED transforms Leer’s council chambers with 135-inch display
Article Text: PPDS, the exclusive global provider of Philips Professional Displays, has announced that the Philips Unite LED 6000 Series All In One solution has been installed at the municipal government offices in Leer, Germany. The 135-inch display is designed to elevate presentations, council sessions, and hybrid collaboration, delivering a high-performance visual experience for local government operations.The Leer Municipal team, based in the historic Rathaus, sought to modernize its central meeting room with a solution that combined reliability, usability, and sustainability. After extensive evaluations, the Philips Unite LED 6000 Series was selected for its outstanding image quality, energy efficiency, and ease of installation.With Full HD resolution, 900 cd/m² brightness, and a fine 1.5mm pixel pitch, the display ensures crystal-clear visuals even in bright environments. Numbers, charts, and detailed documents are presented with exceptional clarity, enhancing transparency and efficiency in council work.The Unite LED solution offers rich color depth, wide viewing angles, and durable design, making it ideal for demanding municipal spaces.Connectivity options include HDMI, DisplayPort, USB-C, and audio, while the integrated media player and wall mounting system simplify deployment. Supporting Leer’s sustainability goals, the display consumes less energy than comparable models, with just 0.5 watts in ultra-low standby mode. This reduces operational costs and environmental impact, aligning with long-term efficiency targets.The installation, managed by LS IT & Media, also integrates a professional AVer PTZ camera, Sennheiser TeamConnect Ceiling microphones, a conferencing PC, and Dante audio technology. Together, these components deliver seamless hybrid meeting support, clear voice capture without table microphones, and advanced features such as recording and AI-powered transcription via tucan.ai.Eckhard Groen of LS IT & Media said: “With the Philips 135” Unite LED wall as the visual centrepiece, a modern, high-performance meeting room was created that sets new standards for the Leer Municipal committee work. The high-quality image, combined with professional audio and video technology, enables efficient, transparent, and contemporary council work.”
Article Summary: Philips Unite LED 6000 Series All In One solution has been installed at the municipal government offices in Leer, Germany. The 135-inch display is designed to elevate presentations, council sessions, and hybrid collaboration. The display consumes less energy than comparable models, with just 0.5 watts in ultra-low standby mode.

Article Title: Myntra deploys AI to transform shopping, seller growth, and supply chains
Article Text: Fashion e-commerce platform Myntra has announced a sweeping expansion of its AI capabilities across customer experience, seller enablement, and operational efficiency. The rollout is designed to compress timelines, improve personalization, and accelerate innovation, with measurable gains already visible across the platform.Myntra’s AI-led Contextual Styling Suite now offers real-time look pairings, helping shoppers style complete outfits. Its Size and Fit Intelligence layer covers 85% of eligible apparel, delivering recommendations in under two seconds and explaining why a size works for a shopper. Fit visualisation across body types is being scaled up.The platform’s Active Matrix Display supports creator-led discovery, validating catalog content and routing complex reviews to specialist teams. Today, 90% of monthly active users experience personalised search, reinforcing Myntra’s position as a leader in AI-driven fashion discovery.AI has cut seller onboarding from 10–15 days to under 2, with registration completing within minutes. The Seller Growth Hub provides daily guidance for new sellers, while advanced dashboards and trend insights support scaling brands.Complementing this, Saarthi, Myntra’s AI-led voice intelligence platform, automates partner communications and manages high-volume queries, freeing human teams to focus on complex issues.Myntra’s cataloging infrastructure now produces 400–600 dynamic product videos daily, reducing turnaround from 1 day to 4 hours. Its compliance protocol, Scout, ensures quality guardrails before listings go live.Internally, BIRA (Business Intelligence Retrieval Agent) allows teams to run complex queries in everyday language, cutting workflows from days to minutes. AI has also driven a 40% increase in feature rollout velocity, handling routine testing and diagnostics while engineers focus on architecture.At the supply chain level, simulations that once took 2 days now run in 1 hour. Meanwhile, Meera, Myntra’s conversational support layer, resolves over 30% of standard customer queries, enabling human agents to focus on nuanced cases.Sharon Pais, Head of Myntra, said: “Technology has always been at the core of every journey on Myntra. Our focus remains on strengthening capabilities that give brand partners clear pathways to scale while continuing to make the shopping experience more relevant and confident for customers. For us, AI is not just a layer we have added on top, it sits at the heart of how Myntra operates.”With AI now embedded across discovery, onboarding, and product development, Myntra is positioning itself as a tech-first fashion platform, reshaping how customers shop and how sellers grow. The company’s deep integration of AI into everyday workflows signals a future where fashion e-commerce is faster, more personalised, and more efficient.
Article Summary: Fashion e-commerce platform Myntra has announced a sweeping expansion of its AI capabilities across customer experience, seller enablement, and operational efficiency. The rollout is designed to compress timelines, improve personalization, and accelerate innovation, with measurable gains already visible.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Sundar Pichai: AI is probably the most important thing humanity has ever worked on; I think of it as something more profound than electricity or fire
Article Text:
Google CEO Sundar Pichai



AI already powers everyday Google services

Google CEO Sundar Pichai once said that artificial intelligence (AI) will have a bigger impact on humanity than some of history's greatest inventions. Speaking at a town hall event in 2019, Sundar Pichai said AI is "probably the most important thing humanity has ever worked on" and described it as being "more profound than electricity or fire. " During the discussion, Pichai said AI has the potential to transform society in ways that go beyond earlier technological breakthroughs."AI is one of the most important things humanity is working on. It is more profound than, I dunno, electricity or fire," Pichai said.When interviewer Kara Swisher questioned the comparison, saying, "Fire? Fire is pretty good," Sundar Pichai stood by his view."Well, it kills people, too," he replied. "We have learned to harness fire for the benefits of humanity but we had to overcome its downsides too. So my point is, AI is really important, but we have to be concerned about it."Sundar Pichai then said people already use AI every day, often without realizing it. He then pointed to Google Translate, image search and Google Photos as examples of AI-powered tools used by billions of people.“AI is just making computers more intelligent and being able to do a wide variety of tasks," he said.The Google CEO also said AI could help address some of the world's biggest problems, including healthcare. “AI holds the potential for some of the biggest advances we are going to see," he said, adding that the technology could play a role in solving diseases such as cancer.At the same time, Sundar Pichai then stated that concerns about AI are valid. "It is right to be concerned, absolutely, you have to worry about it otherwise you are not going to solve it," he said.
Article Summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai once said that artificial intelligence (AI) will have a bigger impact on humanity than some of history's greatest inventions. He said AI is "probably the most important thing humanity has ever worked on" The Google CEO also said AI could help address some of the world's biggest problems, including healthcare.

Article Title: San Francisco sends notice to Apple and Google: Remove these apps right away
Article Text:
Profiting from ‘illegal services’



Apple and Google respond to San Francisco notices



The city of San Francisco has issued a formal legal order to tech giants Apple and Google, demanding the immediate removal of dozens of ‘nudify’ apps from their digital storefronts. These controversial artificial intelligence (AI) apps allow users to digitally alter photographs to remove the clothing off the people in them. According to a report, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu announced the action, accusing both companies of hosting, promoting and actively profiting from applications that generate non-consensual intimate deepfake – a practice that targets women and girls.“Apple and Google are profiting off apps that exploit women and girls by generating nonconsensual intimate deepfakes. While the companies cut ties with some problematic apps, Apple and Google have a responsibility to be proactive and vigilant to prevent sexual abuse,” San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu told TechCrunch.The legal notices sent by the City Attorney’s office state that Apple and Google have likely raked in “millions of dollars in fees” by processing payments for these illicit services. Under California law, it is a crime to knowingly facilitate the creation of non-consensual deepfake pornography.Furthermore, legislation passed in 2025 grants victims the right to file civil lawsuits directly against third-party facilitators, including app store operators, that help distribute or monetize this material.Citing the city's investigation, the report says that both tech giants have been “on notice” regarding their role in processing payments for these illegal purchases for nearly a year but failed to fully clean up their platforms.The letters from the City Attorney warn that Apple and Google face severe civil penalties if they do not comply, giving both corporations 28 days to coordinate directly with the city. In response to the order, both companies stated they are taking swift action against the flagged developers:Apple confirmed that ‘nudify’ apps are strictly forbidden under its guidelines. A spokesperson stated: ““We have removed three of the apps in question and are in the process of terminating their developer accounts from our program. We are in contact with four others that need to address policy violations or risk being removed as well.”Google announced that all five specific applications referenced in the city's letter have been suspended from the Google Play store.“When violations are reported to us, we investigate and take swift action, which in the case of these apps has included suspending hundreds of violating apps and restricting related search terms like ‘nudify’ on our store,” the spokesperson added.
Article Summary: San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu issued a formal legal order to tech giants Apple and Google, demanding the immediate removal of dozens of ‘nudify’ apps from their digital storefronts. The apps allow users to digitally alter photographs to remove the clothing off the people in them.

Article Title: Parag Agrawal, whom Elon Musk removed as Twitter CEO, deepens partnership with Google for his $2 billion AI search startup Parallel Web Systems
Article Text:
Why Parallel matters



Parag Agarwal took inspiration from Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella



Parag Agarwal, the former CEO of Twitter (now X) ousted by Elon Musk in 2022, is now strengthening ties with Google for his AI search startup, Parallel Web Systems, according to a report by Business Insider. Founded over two and a half years ago, Parallel is valued at $2 billion and backed by investors including Sequoia Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins. The report by BI adds that Google will now offer Parallel’s technology to Google Cloud customers building AI agents with the Gemini model. This offers Parallel access to Google’s vast enterprise clientele. Agarwal described the collaboration as “our deepest technical integration with a hyperscaler model lab to date.”Parallel has long built its product on Google Cloud, and engineers from both companies have recently worked to integrate tools so customers don’t need to build connections themselves.Agarwal launched Parallel with the belief that AI agents will eventually search the web far more than humans. Unlike traditional search engines that prioritise readability for people, Parallel retrieves information for AI models including data buried deep within documents.This process, known as grounding, ensures AI agents have access to accurate, up-to-date information.Parallel competes with startups like Exa, which are also tackling the grounding challenge. Google Cloud president Matt Renner said the partnership reflects Google’s strategy of giving customers choice, noting that while Google has its own grounding tools, enterprises may prefer different products depending on their needs.Parallel has already landed major clients such as Harvey, a legal AI startup that uses Parallel’s search tools to combine current web information with internal data. The partnership with Google is expected to accelerate adoption across industries where AI agents are becoming central to workflows.In an interview, Parag Agrawal revealed that when he stepped into the high-profile CEO role at Twitter, he looked at Nadella as a blueprint for driving massive institutional change. "When I took the job at Twitter, I was really inspired by Satya, and I used to call him the founder – or a re-founder, or whatever you want to call that," Agrawal shared in the interview earlier this year.Parag Agrawal explained that taking over the top job at Twitter forced him to figure out how the social media giant could evolve under his leadership and how it could differentiate itself in a crowded tech market.“When I was taking on this Twitter CEO job, it was this moment where I had to figure out what this company was going to become with me and how it was going to be different and the inspiration I got on someone like completely changing a company a business a culture a product came from Satya and I thought of wanting to be a founder in that mold of a founder in that moment,” he said.
Article Summary: Parag Agarwal, the former CEO of Twitter (now X), is strengthening ties with Google for his AI search startup, Parallel Web Systems. Google will now offer Parallel’s technology to Google Cloud customers building AI agents with the Gemini model. This offers Parallel access to Google's vast enterprise clientele. Founded over two and a half years ago, Parallel is valued at $2 billion.

Article Title: Starting this mid-July, Apple is banning from advertising on Apple Maps plumbers, electricians, Cryptocurrency ATMs and ...
Article Text:
Which businesses will be affected due to this new Apple Maps ads policy

Apple has updated its advertising policies to prohibit several categories of businesses from advertising on Apple Maps. This includes home service providers such as plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, HVAC companies, pest control services, roofers and general contractors, as well as cryptocurrency ATMs and bail bond services. According to Apple's newly published Apple Advertising Services News and Stocks, Maps, and Sports Programming Policies, the new rules took effect on July 14.The policy update comes as Apple prepares to introduce advertisements in Apple Maps in the US and Canada later this summer. While the company has announced the feature, it has not yet confirmed a launch date.Under the new Maps-specific advertising rules, Apple prohibits ads that "contain or directly or indirectly promotes home services — including but not limited to plumbing, electrical, locksmith, HVAC, pest control, roofing, or general contracting services."The policy also bans advertisements promoting cryptocurrency ATMs and bail bond services.Medical service advertisements have not been banned outright, but Apple says they will be "evaluated on a case-by-case basis."The Maps-specific restrictions appear alongside Apple's broader advertising policies covering News, Stocks, Maps and Sports programming. Those policies also prohibit deceptive advertising, political advertisements, weapons, controlled substances, violence, defamatory content and other restricted categories.Apple previously said Maps advertisements would begin rolling out in the United States and Canada this summer. According to the company, search results in Apple Maps will display a single sponsored business, identified with a small blue halo around its map pin and labelled as an advertisement in the Suggested Places list.Apple also said that information about advertisements users interact with remains on the device and is neither collected by the company nor shared with third parties.In its blog post, Apple wrote, “These policies help explain what is and isn’t allowed when advertising your content with Apple’s advertising services. The following is not intended as legal advice. Adherence isn’t necessarily sufficient to meet the standards of state, provincial, or federal laws. Apple encourages each advertiser and its agency to consult with legal counsel.When advertising within Apple’s video, display, and maps advertising inventory, you must comply with all the following policies relating to advertising. If you violate any of these policies, you may not be eligible to use Apple’s advertising services. Apple reserves the right to modify these policies and reject, approve, or remove any ad content for any reason, at Apple’s sole discretion.”However, the company has not commented on the newly published Maps advertising policies or announced when the advertising programme will become available to businesses.
Article Summary: Apple has updated its advertising policies to prohibit several categories of businesses from advertising on Apple Maps. This includes home service providers such as plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, HVAC companies, pest control services, roofers and general contractors. The policy update comes as Apple prepares to introduce advertisements in Apple Maps in the US and Canada later this summer.
